reasoning_model_1: Model 1 strictly adheres to the requested script '哇！我赢了！太棒了！' with perfect content accuracy. The core requirement to speak in a 'screaming way' (带尖叫的方式) is executed flawlessly. From 00:00 to 00:03, the voice is high-pitched, loud, and energetic, simulating a genuine shout of excitement. The prosody sounds natural for a person shouting in joy, with appropriate elongation of vowels and intensity. It successfully integrates the paralinguistic feature of screaming into the speech without compromising clarity.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 completely fails on multiple levels. Firstly, regarding Content Accuracy, it does not stick to the script '哇！我赢了！太棒了！'. Instead, it hallucinates a long paragraph of text (00:00-00:10), adding fillers ('嗯嗯'), repetitions ('太棒了太棒了...'), and completely new sentences ('我简直要飞起来了', '这感觉太爽了吧'). Secondly, regarding the Paralinguistic Compliance, it fails to perform the 'screaming' style. Instead of screaming, the voice remains at a normal conversational volume. Worse, at 00:07-00:08, it literally reads out the instruction/description '尖叫尖叫尖叫' (scream scream scream) as text, which is a catastrophic instruction-following error. Due to the severe content deviation and the failure to apply the vocal style, this receives a 0.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It followed the script exactly and successfully modified its vocal style to a high-pitched scream as requested. Model 2 failed completely; it hallucinated a large amount of text, did not scream (it spoke normally), and erroneously read the word 'scream' out loud instead of performing the action. Model 2's output is unusable.
